name: Empirical and Econometric Methods
semantic_meaning: Employs observational data and quasi-experimental designs to identify causal effects, control for unobserved heterogeneity, and address endogeneity in economic and political research.

name: Computational, Data Science, and Machine Learning
semantic_meaning: Utilizes advanced algorithms, natural language processing (NLP), large language models (LLMs), and network simulations to extract patterns, classify unstructured text, and model dynamic network structures.

name: Theoretical and Formal Modeling
semantic_meaning: Develops mathematical frameworks, game-theoretic interactions, and spatial general-equilibrium models to conceptualize strategic behavior, cognitive processes, and structural dynamics within markets and political economies.

name: Experimental Methods
semantic_meaning: Employs randomized controlled trials (RCTs), lab games, and conjoint survey experiments to directly test behavioral responses, evaluate causal policy impacts, and elicit true human valuations under controlled conditions.

name: Qualitative, Descriptive, and Mixed Methods
semantic_meaning: Synthesizes historical context, in-depth interviews, narrative histories, and systematic reviews to provide deep institutional insight, trace complex policy processes, and construct rigorous conceptual definitions.
